rss.title: Prioritising AI data centres could block new homes, builders warn

arxiv_papers.title: The Rise of AI in Weather and Climate Information and its Impact on Global Inequality
arxiv_papers.snippet: The rapid adoption of AI in Earth system science promises unprecedented speed and fidelity in the generation of climate information. arxiv_papers.title: Climate Downscaling with Stochastic Interpolants (CDSI)
arxiv_papers.snippet: Global climate projections rely on computationally demanding Earth System Models (ESMs), which are typically limited to coarse spatial resolutions due to their high cost. podcast:
[Return of the Flesh-Eaters] If a species is horrible enough, do we have the right to kill it forever?
Seventy years ago, a nightmare parasite feasted on the live flesh of warm-blooded creatures in North America: the screwworm. That is, until a young scientist named Edward F.
